summary:
The patient has a history of prior non-small cell lung cancer treated successfully but remains symptomatic with prolonged, worsening back pain—particularly at night—which may indicate underlying malignancy rather than benign structural issues. Evidence supports that post-treatment NSCLC carries risks of distant relapse including skeletal involvement manifesting primarily through lytic bone lesions visible on radiographic exams; these findings correlate well clinically with his symptoms suggesting possible metastatic progression requiring urgent investigation.

Note: This summary focuses only on directly related medical facts derived from available information regarding oncologic follow-up considerations and typical presentation patterns associated with metastatic spinal pathology due to former cancers — excluding irrelevant details unrelated to this specific case context.

Final Answer: $ \boxed{\text{B}} $
